subject: Markets Brief - 2026-06-14
html:
Markets Brief — Sunday 14 June 2026

Prepared: 11:53 BST  |  For: Goldman Sachs Sales & Trading, London  |  Week: BoJ hike Tue · FOMC Wed · BoE Thu · Juneteenth Fri

Weekend Binary: Trump says Iran deal signs TODAY. Tehran disputes exact timeline (“coming days”). Oil & risk assets have largely pre-priced a deal. Monday open direction = Iran outcome overnight.

Key Levels at a Glance

AssetLevelMove / Note
S&P 5007,431+0.5% Friday
Nasdaq25,889+0.3% Friday
Dow Jones51,202+0.7% Friday
FTSE 100~10,472+1.6% Friday
DAX~24,635+1.8% Friday
Nikkei 22566,078+2.9% Friday
UST 2yr4.09%—
UST 10yr4.50%+3bps Friday; 2s10s -41bps
Bund 10yr3.00%Easing from post-ECB peak of 3.05%
Gilt 10yr~4.75% est.BoE event risk Thursday
DXY99.8015-month lows
EUR/USD1.1650—
GBP/USD1.3430—
USD/JPY158.95-0.5%; BoJ hike 97% priced
Brent$87.33/bbl-4.0% Friday; -20% from 2026 highs
WTI~$83.00/bbl~$4 discount to Brent
Gold (spot)$4,224/ozResilient; defying risk-on
Copper (LME)$6.43/lb (~$14,175/t)+2.7% Friday; recovering from 3-wk lows
BTC$64,385Bounced from <$60k early June
ETH~$1,671—

1. Overnight Market Wrap

Equities — Strong Friday; DM closed weekend

Friday June 12 was a broad rally on two converging catalysts: (1) SpaceX SPCX Nasdaq debut — the largest IPO in history ($75bn raised), closing its first session +19% at $161; and (2) growing confidence an Iran peace framework is imminent. S&P 500 +0.5% to 7,431 (third consecutive up-week), Dow +0.7% to 51,202, Nasdaq +0.3% to 25,889. Europe outperformed: FTSE 100 +1.6%, DAX +1.8% — oil deflation is proportionally a larger tailwind for energy-importing European corporates. Nikkei 225 was the global standout: +2.9% to 66,078, boosted by Iran peace optimism and weak yen supporting exporter margins.

No developed-market sessions over the weekend. ES futures pre-open tonight will be the first price signal; gap direction entirely driven by Iran deal confirmation or breakdown.

Rates — Quiet; heavy-event week ahead

UST 2yr at 4.09%, 10yr at 4.50% — curve inverted ~41bps but far flatter than 2023’s 100bps+ inversion. Friday saw a modest bear-flattener (+3bps 10yr) as pre-FOMC positioning begins. Bund 10yr at 3.00%, easing from the post-ECB hike peak of 3.05% — markets beginning to price the June 11 hike as potentially the last of this ECB cycle.

FX — Dollar soft; yen near intervention zone

DXY at 99.80, near 15-month lows. Dollar under broad pressure from: Iran deal reducing energy-inflation expectations; risk-on reducing safe-haven demand; BoJ rate hike expectations trimming the US-Japan rate differential. USDJPY at 158.95, off its 160 intervention-risk highs. A confirmed Iran deal could push EUR/USD through 1.17 as the ECB’s case for further hikes diminishes rapidly.

Commodities — Oil in freefall; metals recovering

Brent fell over 4% on Friday to $87.33/bbl — lowest since early March; down ~20% from 2026 highs. The draft MOU commits Tehran to reopening Hormuz within 30 days of signing. Physical normalization takes months — Fitch models Hormuz reopening end-July. Gold at $4,224/oz, holding on soft DXY and structural central-bank buying. Copper at $6.43/lb recovered +2.7% Friday from 3-week lows as Iran peace optimism improved the EM growth and industrial metals demand outlook.

Crypto

BTC at $64,385, recovering from a dip below $60,000 in early June. ETH ~$1,671. Crypto tracking equity risk-on/risk-off closely. SpaceX IPO capital absorption ($75bn) may constrain marginal crypto flows near-term.

2. The Day Ahead — Week of 15–20 June 2026

Date / Time (BST)EventConsensus / Note
Sun 14 JuneIran deal signing windowTrump: “today”; Tehran: “coming days” — watch overnight
Mon 15 JuneMarkets reopen; gap on Iran outcomeBoJ meeting Day 1
Mon 15, 13:30US NY Empire State Manufacturing (June)—
Tue 16 June, ~03:00BoJ Rate Decision (June 15–16)97% priced for +25bps → 1.0%; highest since 1995
Wed 17 June, 07:00UK CPI — May 2026 (ONS)Key BoE input; April was 2.8%; watch services
Wed 17, 13:30US Retail Sales (May) + Industrial ProductionLeading consumption and output read
Wed 17 June, 19:00FOMC Rate Decision98.3% hold at 3.50–3.75%; Warsh’s FIRST meeting
Wed 17, ~19:30Warsh press conference + dot plot + SEPHike signal? Dot plot scrapped? THE event this week
Thu 18 June, 12:00BoE MPC DecisionHold at 3.75% expected; watch vote split
Thu 18, 13:30US Initial Jobless Claims + Housing Starts—
Fri 19 JuneJuneteenth — US markets closedThin global liquidity Friday

Speakers in blackout: Fed (since May 28), ECB (quiet period), BoE (quiet period).

3. What’s Moving Markets

1. US-Iran Nuclear Deal — The Event of 2026 CRITICAL

Trump said Sunday the deal would be signed “today.” Tehran’s FM pushed back, saying it “will not be tomorrow” as of Saturday. Both sides confirmed a 14-point draft MOU brokered by Pakistani mediators and finalized June 12. Key terms: Iran never pursues nuclear weapons; suspends enrichment in exchange for sanctions relief; Hormuz reopens within 30 days of signing. A 60-day MOU framework, not a final treaty — reversibility risk exists. Qatari mediators were in Tehran over the weekend to finalise.

Markets have already priced a deal heavily (Brent -20% from 2026 highs). Important nuances not fully processed: physical Hormuz clearance takes weeks; Iranian production ramp from idled fields takes months; the 60-day MOU is reversible. Fitch estimates Hormuz physically reopens end-July. If deal delays or collapses, oil snaps back through $95 — the biggest single tail risk heading into the week.

2. SpaceX SPCX — Biggest IPO in History, Now a $2tn Company NEW

SpaceX priced at $135/share on June 12, raising $75bn — eclipsing Saudi Aramco’s $29.4bn record. SPCX opened at $150, surged to $176.52 intraday, and closed at $161 (+19%), valuing it above $2tn. Revenue $18.7bn last year on a $4.2bn operating loss — market paying for Starlink broadband optionality. Watch Monday: (a) day-two momentum vs profit-taking from day-one allocations; (b) S&P 500 inclusion path (profitability hurdle not yet met, but a structural catalyst if resolved); (c) Musk commentary. The $75bn capital absorption is large enough to constrain marginal risk appetite elsewhere.

3. FOMC June 17 — Warsh’s First Meeting ACTIVE

Kevin Warsh chairs his first FOMC on Wednesday. Rate hold (3.50–3.75%) is 98.3% priced. The real event is the dot plot and press conference: Does the dot introduce hike language for late 2026? Does Warsh abandon the easing bias? Rumours he may scrap the dot plot entirely — which would be a communication-philosophy signal in its own right. Warsh has flagged Fed “complacency” on inflation (CPI ~4.2% YoY). A hawkish surprise is the clearest scenario for a crowded-position unwind (long gold, short USD, long equities). Key reflexes: 2yr UST, SOFR Dec 2026, gold.

4. BoJ June 15–16 — Hiking to 1.0%, Highest Since 1995 IMMINENT TUE

BoJ expected to raise +25bps to 1.0% at Tuesday’s announcement (~03:00 BST) — 97% market probability and the highest Japanese rates in nearly 30 years. Macro: core CPI 2.8% fiscal 2026, growth revised to 0.5%. The global risk is the yen carry trade: USDJPY at 158.95 (already down from ~167 at the conflict peak) means much is priced, but aggressive forward guidance could trigger a sharp JPY surge and carry unwind draining global liquidity — particularly EM assets. With SPCX having absorbed $75bn of capital, any additional liquidity drain is amplified.

5. Bank of England June 18 — Hold, Eyes on UK CPI ACTIVE

BoE expected to hold at 3.75% Thursday. April MPC vote was 8-1 (one hawk for immediate hike). UK CPI for May released Wednesday (07:00 BST) — April was 2.8%; BoE forecasts 3.1% Q2 rising to 3.3% Q3. If Wednesday CPI prints above 3%, the vote split likely widens and GBP rallies. Iran deal deflation wildcard: if deal signs before Wednesday, BoE’s inflation path looks less dire and hawks may pull back. GBPUSD at 1.3430 has a hawkish BoE partially priced.

6. ECB — First Hike Since 2023, Now Facing Iran Deflation ACTIVE

ECB raised deposit rate 25bps to 2.25% on June 11 — first hike since 2023. EZ CPI 3.2%, core 2.5%; Q1 GDP -0.2% (stagflation configuration). Lagarde declined to pre-commit to further hikes. The June 11 hike may look premature if Iran deal takes energy out of the CPI trajectory. Bund 10yr at 3.00% has started pricing this (peaked 3.05%, now easing). ECB terminal rate will reprice sharply lower on a confirmed deal — EUR/USD upside from 1.1650 toward 1.18 is substantial.

7. Copper — Recovering Toward Record Highs STRUCTURAL BID

LME copper at $6.43/lb ($14,175/t), +2.7% Friday from 3-week lows. Goldman estimates 330,000t supply deficit 2026. AI data-centre copper intensity (~12x more per MW than conventional), EV manufacturing, and grid electrification are the demand drivers. Chinese smelters buying pyrite as concentrate substitute — a sign of genuine tightness. Mining stocks: COPX ETF +3.38%, BHP +3.21%, Antofagasta +3.10% Friday. Cochilco raised 2026 copper forecast to ~$5.55/lb; current spot at $6.43/lb reflects the supply-deficit premium. The structural story is not derailed by an Iran deal.

8. Gold — Holding Through the Geopolitical Premium Reduction WATCH FOMC

Gold at $4,224/oz, resilient. The Iran deal reduces the geopolitical safe-haven bid, but three structural supports remain: (1) DXY at 99.80; (2) persistent EM central-bank buying (China-led); (3) capped real-rate expectations. Warsh’s dot plot Wednesday is the key near-term binary — a hike signal spikes real rates and triggers a sell-off from a crowded net-long. Watch gold as a real-time barometer of the Warsh surprise magnitude.

4. Desk Angle — Flows, Vol & Positioning

Three binary events in five trading days — BoJ Tuesday, Warsh FOMC Wednesday, BoE Thursday — layered on the weekend Iran deal binary. Base cases are largely priced; the tails are where flow lives.

  • Iran / Oil: Short energy is consensus and crowded. Confirmed deal extends toward $80 Brent; any delay or collapse gaps oil back above $92 and flushes the short book. This is the asymmetric risk event of the week, not FOMC.
  • FOMC: Pre-FOMC positioning is long gold, long equities, short front-end UST, short USD. Warsh hawkish surprise (hike dot) = 2yr UST -10 to -20bps, USD rallies, gold drops $100-150. Clearest crowded-unwind scenario.
  • BoJ: 97% hike priced. Tail risks: (i) Hold = massive JPY selloff, Nikkei melt-up; (ii) Hike + aggressive guidance = USDJPY to 155+, carry unwind, VIX spike. SPCX $75bn capital absorption amplifies any liquidity drain.
  • GBP event stack: UK CPI Wed + BoE Thu = most event-dense currency of the week. CFTC longs building. If CPI > 3% and BoE vote shifts to 7-2 hawks, cable could rip 1-1.5 big figures. Iran deflation wildcard is the counter.

Positioning checklist: Short energy (crowded; stops at $92 Brent) · Long European equities vs short E&P (still early innings) · Short USD/JPY (base case well set; watch BoJ guidance) · Long gold into FOMC (modest size; Warsh is the risk) · 2yr UST receivers (good carry into neutral FOMC; stop on hike dot) · SPCX day-two momentum (S&P 500 inclusion optionality is long-dated but real).

5. Story Follow-Ups

ST-001 Iran Deal: CRITICAL Not confirmed signed 11:53 BST Sunday. Timeline slippage is primary risk. Watching for overnight confirmation.

ST-002 ECB Hike: ACTIVE 2.25% delivered June 11. Iran deal would reduce case for further hikes. Bund 10yr easing to 3.00%.

ST-003 FOMC: ACTIVE Decision Wednesday 19:00 BST. 98.3% hold. Dot plot/Warsh tone is the event. Rumour: dot plot may be scrapped.

ST-004 BoE: ACTIVE Thursday 12:00 BST. Hold expected at 3.75%. UK CPI Wednesday 07:00 BST is key.

ST-005 Gold $4,224: ACTIVE Holding. Warsh dot plot Wednesday is the near-term binary. Crowded net-long into FOMC.

ST-006 Copper $6.43/lb: ACTIVE Recovered +2.7% Friday. Supply deficit intact. Mining stocks leading.

ST-007 Nikkei 66,078: ACTIVE BoJ hike Tuesday is immediate event. USDJPY and carry trade reaction are key.

ST-008 BoJ June 16 (NEW): IMMINENT 97% probability +25bps to 1.0% — highest since 1995. Decision ~03:00 BST Tuesday. Carry trade unwind risk globally.

ST-009 SpaceX SPCX (NEW): NEW Closed +19% at $161 on Day 1 ($75bn raised). Re-opens Monday. Day-two action and S&P 500 inclusion path to watch.

6. Trade Ideas

Illustrative only — not investment advice. For desk discussion only.

1. Long EUR/USD — Target 1.18, Stop 1.155
Iran deal confirmation removes the energy inflation that justified ECB’s June 11 hike; ECB terminal rate reprices sharply lower. DXY at 99.80 breaking support. EUR/USD at 1.1650 has room to 1.18 if Warsh is neutrally-hawkish. Risk: Warsh hawkish surprise; Iran deal collapse.
2. Short Brent Aug Futures — Target $80, Stop $92
Draft MOU commits Iran to Hormuz reopening within 30 days. Production normalization adds supply over 3–6 months. A clean signing prints $80 over 4–6 weeks. Saudi relief valve could accelerate. Risk: Deal collapse; OPEC emergency cut.
3. Short USD/JPY — Target 155, Stop 161.50
BoJ hike to 1.0% Tuesday narrows US-Japan rate differential. Neutrally-hawkish Warsh (base case) means differential compression isn’t offset by USD strength. 155 is achievable on the week. Risk: Warsh far more hawkish; BoJ surprise hold.
4. Long European Consumer Discretionary vs Short European Oil & Gas (SX8P/SX3P)
Iran deal is an asymmetric tailwind for energy-importing European consumers and a headwind for E&P names. Friday already showed this in sector moves; still early innings if Brent goes to $80. Risk: Iran delay; EZ recession overshadows rotation.
